aldenheim: Aldenheim was a major city in Kinsweave that fell three centuries ago when House Buarath seized control of the EarthForge beneath it. What remains are ruins occupied by Buarath sentinels who control access to the underground forge complex. Before the fall, Aldenheim was a center of learning and elemental research, housing the largest pre-Deceiver archive on the continent.
earthforge: The EarthForge is an ancient elemental forge beneath Aldenheim, built by the Elemental Lords during the Dawn era. It can transmute raw elemental material into any substance. House Buarath has controlled it for 300 years and uses it to produce rare materials. The activation sequence is known only to a handful of living scholars.
house_buarath: House Buarath is a noble house in Kinsweave that serves Buaress the Many-Mask. They seized the EarthForge 300 years ago and have systematically destroyed historical records of pre-Buarath Aldenheim. Their sentinels patrol the ruins and discourage archaeological investigation.
sand_wardens: The Sand Wardens are a patrol and escort order operating throughout the Infinite Desert. They maintain the pilgrim roads between oasis cities and protect caravans from desert creatures and bandits. Membership requires surviving a solo crossing of the Glass Wastes -- a dead zone where Kin-sense goes silent.
trade_houses: The Trade Houses are seven mercantile guilds based in the Twin Rivers region. They control most commerce in the northern hemisphere and have significant political influence in Custos do Aeternos. The seven houses compete fiercely but present a united front against outside interference.
kin_sense: Kin-sense is the elemental sixth sense shared by all Kin races. It detects the elemental resonance of nearby presences within 60 feet, passes through barriers up to 5 feet thick, and cannot be dispelled or suppressed by anti-magic. It is NOT magic. Nine resonance categories exist: harmonic (Kin), wild_static (Tanuki, beasts), warm_static (plants, insects), breach_static (Goblins, Wargs), null (Silentborn), void (Domnathar), echo (dreaming presences), none (undead, constructs, Farborn), and raw_static (pure elementals).
elemental_lords: The four Elemental Lords shaped Oreka: the Lord of Stone (now comatose, dreaming beneath the mountains), the Lady of Fire (active, volatile, patron of the Rarozhki Dwarves), the Lady of the Sea (active, patient, patron of the Eruskan Humans and Pekakarlik Dwarves), and the Youngest Brother -- the Wind Lord (exiled, his return prophesied). They do not directly intervene in mortal affairs but their influence flows through elemental resonance, Kin-sense, and elemental zones.
the_unstrung: The Unstrung is a 600-year-old criminal organization with roots in the Deceiver's era. They operate throughout all seven regions using the Deceiver's Feat -- the ability to suppress one's Kin-sense signature, becoming invisible to detection. The organization never leaves debts unpaid in either direction.
kinsweave: Kinsweave is the north-central region of Oreka, characterized by highland quarries, haunted ruins, and pilgrimage routes. It is the site of the Aldenheim ruins and the contested Stonefall quarries. The region has a complicated relationship with House Buarath, which controls significant territory. Six ruins dot the highlands, each with their own dangers and secrets.
infinite_desert: The Infinite Desert is the equatorial belt of Oreka, a vast expanse of volcanic glass wastes, sand seas, and isolated oasis cities connected by pilgrim roads maintained by the Sand Wardens. The city of Kharazhad (population 20,000) is its largest settlement, built around volcanic forges. The Glass Wastes are dead zones where Kin-sense goes completely silent.
twin_rivers: The Twin Rivers region is the northwest of Oreka -- the most densely populated area on the continent. Two great rivers converge at Custos do Aeternos (population 25,000), the largest city. The region is dominated by Trade House commerce, river trade, and canopy cities built in the ancient forests. Liraveth, Aerithal, Greenford, and Stoneharbor are major settlements.
gatefall_reach: Gatefall Reach is the southeastern frontier of Oreka, defined by the Silence Breach -- an expanding zone where Kin-sense becomes unreliable or goes dead. Wind-Riders patrol the frontier. The Tomb of Kings lies at the edge of the Breach, where the Gatefall Remnant stands watch. Something is moving in the Breach, and the frontier towns of Hillwatch, Silkenbough, and Glimmerholt are on edge.
deepwater_marches: The Deepwater Marches are the southwestern region -- dense jungle, river networks, and the massive city of Titan's Rest (population 28,000). Warg settlements exist in the deep jungle, freed servitors who have built their own civilization. The region runs on intelligence trade and river commerce. Canopy Hold, Thornwall, and Murtavah Root are key outposts.
silence_breach: The Silence Breach is an expanding anomaly in Gatefall Reach where Kin-sense becomes unreliable or fails entirely. Its origin is unknown. Creatures from within the Breach register as 'none' on Kin-sense -- complete absence, like undead or constructs, but they are neither. The Gatefall Remnant and frontier wardens monitor its expansion.
ascended_gods: The Ascended Gods were once mortal Kin who achieved apotheosis after the Fall of Aldenheim. Nine are recognized: Dagdan the United Sun, Hareem the Golden Rose, Kaile'a (mariners), Cinvarin the Five Witnesses (unity), Semyon (healing), Apela Kelsoe (freedom), Ludus Galerius (exploration), Gonmareck Ritler (smithing), and The Hand Unanswered (compassion). Players can pray at their shrines for deity-specific blessings.
oreka_overview: Oreka is a continent shaped by four Elemental Lords -- Stone, Fire, Sea, and Wind. Two parallel Giant's Teeth mountain ranges flank an equatorial desert corridor, dividing civilization into northern and southern hemispheres. Seven regions span the continent, each with distinct cultures, hazards, and politics. All Kin share elemental resonance through Kin-sense -- a sixth sense that defines who belongs and who does not. The world's history turns on the Fall of Aldenheim, the rise of the Ascended Gods, and the slow expansion of the Silence Breach.
frontier: The frontier refers to the border regions of Gatefall Reach where civilization thins and the Silence Breach looms. Wardens, scouts, and Wind-Riders patrol the edge. Towns like Hillwatch, Brayholt, and Redtrail are the last outposts before the unknown. The frontier attracts adventurers, refugees, and those with something to prove.
